title: After a Bitter Split, European Leaders Play Nice With Trump
summary: A peace framework with Iran, and hope for cooperation with Ukraine, softened the tone on Tuesday at a Group of 7 gathering in France.

title: Who Are the Leaders at the G7 Summit in France?
summary: On a roll or against a wall, Group of 7 leaders bring sharply different agendas. The leaders of some other nations are also attending to press their own interests.

title: The Iran War Permanently Altered the Global Economy
summary: The global order has been altered, and economies are unlikely to simply pick up where they left off before the U.S. and Israel began bombing Iran.

title: After U.S. Strike on Iranian School, Months Pass Without Answers
summary: U.S. officials have not publicly acknowledged responsibility for the deaths or released a report on their findings from an investigation into the Feb. 28 strike.

title: War Hangs Over American Farmers as Fertilizer Prices Rise
summary: The Iran war and lower crop prices have hurt makers of sulfur-based supplements. But farmers won’t skip nitrogen-based fertilizers, no matter the cost.

title: How Our Reporters Got at the Truth of Jeffrey Epstein’s Death
summary: Four Times journalists talk about their investigation into Epstein’s final weeks in jail and the unanswered questions that their reporting tackles.

title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
summary: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.

title: Why Is Everyone Waiting Hours for Frozen Yogurt? Why Am I?
summary: The yogurt is not special. Let’s just get that out of the way now.

title: Claiming an Antifa Plot, U.S. Charges 15 in Minneapolis With Conspiracy
summary: The 94-page conspiracy indictment was filed as federal prosecutors in Minnesota had trouble sustaining many criminal cases filed against Immigration and Customs Enforcement protesters.

title: Trump Breaks Up Education Dept., Prompting Worries Over Civil Rights
summary: Special education programs and the civil rights office will be moved out of the Education Department, the most aggressive move yet by the Trump administration to dismantle the agency.

title: At Least 5 Charged in Alleged Plot to Attack White House During U.F.C. Event
summary: Charging documents outlined an ambitious plan involving explosive-laden drones and rifles, but left less clear that the conspirators had the means to carry it out.

title: Justice Dept. Considering Death Penalty for Alleged National Guard Shooter
summary: Federal prosecutors told a judge that they were deciding whether to seek the death penalty if Rahmanullah Lakanwal is convicted of killing a National Guard specialist.

title: Takeaways From JD Vance’s New Book ‘Communion,’ on Faith and His Political Ambitions
summary: In a new memoir, Mr. Vance recalls an “unsettling” meeting with Vatican officials on immigration policy and disavows his infamous disparagement of some Democrats as “childless cat ladies.”

title: Vance Is Pressed on Epstein, Race and His Boss on ‘The View’
summary: Vice President JD Vance is promoting a new book on faith and facing questions about his own presidential ambitions.

title: Ebola Outbreak Could Become Worst on Record, Africa C.D.C. Chief Warns
summary: Health workers have warned that the outbreak, already one of the worst in decades, could take as long as a year to contain if infection rates do not flatten.

title: SpaceX Is More Valuable Than Meta, Broadcom and Many Others
summary: In its first few days of trading, Elon Musk’s newly listed rocket maker has become one of the world’s most valuable companies.

title: D.O.J. Seeks to Halt Pollution Lawsuit Against Elon Musk’s Data Center
summary: The department cited national security concerns, saying Elon Musk’s company had played a crucial role in the Iran war. It also argued it has the authority to stop environmental lawsuits brought by citizens.

title: Riding High After I.P.O., SpaceX Will Buy A.I. Start-Up for $60 Billion
summary: SpaceX exercised its option to acquire Cursor in an all-stock deal, bolstering Elon Musk’s ambitions in artificial intelligence.

title: These Dating Apps Want You to Meet at the Gym
summary: A slew of new apps focus on connecting people who love to work out.

title: What to Watch in Primary and Runoff Elections in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
summary: The top race of the day is in Georgia, where Republican voters will pick a nominee to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff, a Democrat.

title: Montana Democrats Are Divided Over How to Win a Republican-held Senate Seat
summary: A rift in Montana between the Democratic nominee and an independent candidate could boost Republican hopes of holding on to an open seat in conservative territory.

title: Are You a Morning Person or Night Owl? Take Our Sleep Quiz.
summary: Think you’re a morning person, a night owl or something in between? Take our “chronotype” quiz to find out.

title: Iran Found Trump’s Bone Spur
summary: In a contest of wills, the hard men of Tehran prevailed over the vain man of Washington.

title: Graham Platner, Jon Ossoff and the New Rules of Political Attention
summary: Chris Hayes and Ezra Klein discuss how the Democrats are winning the battle for attention, and whether that can translate into winning elections.

title: Federal Judge Blocks Idaho Law Criminalizing Transgender Bathroom Use
summary: Six transgender residents claimed that the bathroom ban, the most restrictive in the nation, violated their constitutional rights.
